title: NIH/3T3 maintenance (ATCC)
description: Adherent NIH/3T3 (ATCC CRL-1658) in DMEM + 10% CALF bovine serum (not FBS); subculture at 80% confluence or less. Source: ATCC https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1658

payload.description: Routine maintenance of adherent NIH/3T3 mouse embryonic fibroblasts per the ATCC CRL-1658 product sheet. Base medium: DMEM (ATCC 30-2002). IMPORTANT: ATCC recommends 10% CALF bovine serum (ATCC 30-2031), not fetal bovine serum, because fetal serum promotes spontaneous transformation and loss of contact inhibition (the product sheet's complete-medium line lists 10% FBS, but calf serum is the depositor-recommended practice). Subculture at least twice per week at 80% confluence or less and never let the cells become confluent. Reseed at 3 to 5 x 10^3 cells/cm^2. Rinse with D-PBS, then dissociate with 0.25% (w/v) trypsin / 0.53 mM EDTA, 5 to 15 min. 37 C, 5% CO2. Doubling time is not published, so it is omitted. Source: ATCC CRL-1658 https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1658

cell_line.name: NIH/3T3
cell_line.notes: ATCC CRL-1658. Grow at 37 C, 5% CO2. Serum is 10% CALF bovine serum (ATCC 30-2031), NOT FBS, to preserve contact inhibition. Do not let cells reach confluence. Source: https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1658

media.base_medium: Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's Medium (DMEM), ATCC 30-2002; serum is calf bovine serum (ATCC 30-2031), not FBS

planned_events.notes: Seed day. Rinse with D-PBS, briefly rinse with 0.25% trypsin / 0.53 mM EDTA, add 2 to 3 mL Trypsin-EDTA, disperse 5 to 15 min, quench, count, and reseed at 3 to 5 x 10^3 cells/cm^2. Do not shake or knock the flask.
Check that cells are still sub-confluent. Subculture before they reach confluence.
Subculture at 80% confluence or less, at least twice per week. Never let the cells become confluent.
